name: Oslo
profile: Norway's capital and largest city — government, finance and banking, technology and startups, shipping and maritime services, energy, media and research, anchored by the University of Oslo and Oslo University Hospital. The country's deepest and most international job market.

name: Bergen
profile: Norway's second city, on the west coast — a global centre for maritime industries, offshore energy, seafood and aquaculture, and marine research, with the University of Bergen, Haukeland University Hospital, and a historic Hanseatic port.

name: Trondheim
profile: Norway's technology and research capital — home to NTNU (the country's largest university for science and engineering), SINTEF, St. Olavs Hospital, and clusters in software, medical technology, marine science and clean energy.

name: Stavanger
profile: Norway's energy capital on the south-west coast — the headquarters of the oil and gas industry, offshore and subsea engineering, and a growing base in offshore wind and energy transition, with the University of Stavanger and Stavanger University Hospital.

name: Tromsø
profile: The largest city in northern Norway and a gateway to the Arctic — Arctic and marine research, fisheries and aquaculture, space and climate science, and healthcare, anchored by UiT The Arctic University of Norway and the University Hospital of North Norway.
